section: AP 15: Hematopathology Topics for Anatomic Pathology Residents
allocation_strategy: Clinical judgment prioritizing major hematologic malignancies, morphologic recognition, and essential diagnostic skills

questions.topic: Diffuse large B-cell lymphoma: morphologic variants
questions.education_objective: Recognize DLBCL morphologic patterns and apply diagnostic criteria
Grade follicular lymphoma and understand prognostic implications

questions.topic: Mantle cell lymphoma: morphology and immunophenotype
questions.education_objective: Recognize mantle cell lymphoma features and confirm with cyclin D1
Identify Burkitt lymphoma morphology and correlate with MYC translocations

questions.topic: CLL/SLL vs. other small B-cell lymphomas
questions.education_objective: Distinguish CLL/SLL from other small lymphocytic proliferations

questions.topic: MALT lymphoma: morphology and H. pylori association
questions.education_objective: Recognize MALT lymphoma features and understand infectious associations

questions.topic: Classical Hodgkin lymphoma: Reed-Sternberg cell variants
questions.education_objective: Identify Reed-Sternberg cells and classify Hodgkin lymphoma subtypes
Distinguish NLPHL from classical Hodgkin lymphoma
Recognize T-ALL/LBL morphology and immunophenotype

questions.topic: Anaplastic large cell lymphoma: ALK+ vs. ALK-
questions.education_objective: Classify ALCL subtypes and correlate ALK status with prognosis
Recognize NK/T-cell lymphoma morphology and EBV association

questions.topic: CML: chronic vs. accelerated vs. blast phase
questions.education_objective: Recognize CML phases and apply diagnostic criteria for progression

questions.topic: Acute promyelocytic leukemia: morphology and PML-RARA
questions.education_objective: Recognize APL morphologic features and understand molecular pathogenesis
Correlate AML morphology with specific cytogenetic abnormalities
Recognize CMML diagnostic criteria and distinguish from other MDS/MPN
Distinguish primary from secondary erythrocytosis using clinical and laboratory data
Recognize myelofibrosis morphologic progression and grading systems
Distinguish plasma cell myeloma from MGUS using morphologic and clinical criteria
Recognize amyloid deposits and understand classification systems

questions.topic: Flow cytometry: B-cell vs. T-cell markers
questions.education_objective: Interpret basic flow cytometry panels for lymphoid lineage determination

questions.topic: Flow cytometry: myeloid markers and blast assessment
questions.education_objective: Apply flow cytometry for myeloid blast identification and immunophenotyping

questions.topic: Iron deficiency anemia: morphology and iron studies
questions.education_objective: Recognize iron deficiency morphology and interpret iron studies

questions.topic: Sickle cell disease: morphology and complications
questions.education_objective: Identify sickle cell morphology and understand clinical complications
Distinguish alpha from beta thalassemia using morphologic and laboratory findings

questions.topic: Megaloblastic anemia: B12 vs. folate deficiency
questions.education_objective: Recognize megaloblastic morphology and distinguish B12 from folate deficiency
Recognize aplastic anemia and distinguish from other causes of pancytopenia

questions.topic: Immune hemolytic anemia: warm vs. cold types
questions.education_objective: Classify immune hemolytic anemias and correlate with laboratory findings
Identify schistocytes and understand MAHA differential diagnosis

questions.topic: TTP vs. HUS: clinical and laboratory distinction
questions.education_objective: Distinguish TTP from HUS using clinical and laboratory criteria
Recognize DIC laboratory patterns and understand underlying mechanisms

questions.topic: von Willebrand disease: types and testing
questions.education_objective: Classify von Willebrand disease types and interpret specialized testing
Recognize ITP and distinguish from other causes of thrombocytopenia
Recognize atypical lymphocytes and correlate with infectious mononucleosis
Identify malaria parasites and distinguish species
Recognize HLH morphologic features and understand diagnostic criteria
Recognize Castleman disease morphology and classify variants
Identify metastatic carcinoma in bone marrow and suggest primary sites
Recognize metastatic carcinoma patterns and determine primary sites
Apply systematic approach to peripheral blood smear evaluation

questions.topic: Bone marrow evaluation: cellularity and morphology
questions.education_objective: Assess bone marrow cellularity and recognize normal vs. abnormal morphology
